content.content: I have added some new files to /user/github/linear_algebra_operation. Please upload these files to GitHub to sync the repository.

<function_thought>
I need to first list the contents of the directory '/user/github/linear_algebra_operation' to identify the new files that have been added. Once I have that information, I can proceed to upload them to GitHub using the git_push function. 
</function_thought>

<function_call>[list_directory(path="/user/github/linear_algebra_operation")]</function_call>

<final_answer></final_answer>

[{'name': '.git_config', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': False, 'execute': False}}, {'name': 'docs', 'type': 'directory', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': True, 'execute': False}}, {'name': 'matrix_ops.py', 'type': 'file', 'permissions': {'read': True, 'write': True, 'execute': False}}]

<function_thought>
The directory '/user/github/linear_algebra_operation' contains the following items:
1. A configuration file: '.git_config'
2. A directory: 'docs'
3. A Python file: 'matrix_ops.py'

Since the relevant files for upload are not marked as hidden or unrelated (i.e., not '.git_config'), I will proceed to push the directory 'docs' and the file 'matrix_ops.py' to GitHub. I will push the entire directory since it may contain new files as well. 
</function_thought>

<function_call>[git_push(local_path="/user/github/linear_algebra_operation", force=False)]</function_call>
